title: Bind issue
description:
executing the following query on (http://54.36.55.224:8892/sparql, virtuoso Version: 07.20.3217 Build: Aug 22 2018), I receive strange results....
PREFIX n0:<http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX n1:<http://semanticweb.org/askelys/>
PREFIX n2:<http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
SELECT DISTINCT ?annee0 ?s ?t WHERE {
?OpenMedic0 n0:type n1:OpenMedic.
?OpenMedic0 n1:annee ?annee0.
  BIND( regex(str(?annee0),"2015","i") AS ?s ).
{
    BIND (regex(str(?annee0),"2015","i") AS ?t).
}
}  LIMIT 1000

results :

e0 | s | t
-- | -- | --
1 | 2014 | "0"^^xsd:integer | "0"^^xsd:integer
2 | 2015 | "1"^^xsd:integer | "0"^^xsd:integer
3 | 2016 | "0"^^xsd:integer | "0"^^xsd:integer
4 | 2017 | "0"^^xsd:integer | "0"^^xsd:integer
5 | 2018 | "0"^^xsd:integer | "0"^^xsd:integer

It's seems that the second bind (encaspuled) have wrong results.
If I replace this second bind directly with a filter (so FILTER(regex(str(?annee0),"2015","i"))), the query works and I receive only 2015 results.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE OpenMedic (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, annee VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE OpenMedicResults (id INT, s INT, t INT, FOREIGN KEY (id) REFERENCES OpenMedic(id));
INSERT INTO OpenMedicResults (id, s, t) SELECT id, CASE WHEN annee LIKE '%2015%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, CASE WHEN annee LIKE '%2015%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM OpenMedic;
SELECT DISTINCT annee, s, t FROM OpenMedicResults WHERE s = 1;
key_bug_features: Second BIND with regex produces incorrect results
